project.name: DomainLease-Linux
project.purpose: Introduce a capability-disciplined execution and resource lease model into Linux scheduler foundations, eventually targeting monitor-backed domain isolation suitable for datacenter OS use. This project was called CapSched-Linux during the private modeling phase.

github_publication.reason: Full local Linux history push failed with GitHub HTTP 500 and is not required for the private delta; patch queue preserves the private commits against the recorded upstream base.

architecture_intent.prototype_track: Linux-only DomainLease-Linux L0 for performance, integration, and semantic exploration. It must not claim hypervisor-grade isolation.
architecture_intent.production_track: Monitor-backed DomainLease-H: Linux-compatible domain-aware kernel plus small Domain Monitor enforcing non-forgeable domain activation, epochs, MemoryView, IOMMU/queue ownership, and root CPU budgets.
architecture_intent.datacenter_goal: Provide process-to-container-granularity domains with isolation strength comparable to VM boundaries, while preserving lower cost and Linux ABI compatibility.
architecture_intent.cluster_goal: Support a cluster-wide capability/resource lease namespace without requiring one monolithic distributed kernel.

core_concepts.Domain: Schedulable protection, ownership, audit, and resource context. May represent a process, service, container, tenant, or cluster cell.
core_concepts.DomainTag: Active protection context selected at context switch; binds domain identity, epoch, capability root, MemoryView, object/device views, and side-channel policy.
core_concepts.RunCap: Authority to submit a specific thread/task for runnable execution. It must not include kill, suspend, priority, budget, affinity, spawn, or mint authority.
core_concepts.SchedContext: Independent CPU-time resource object containing budget, period, remaining runtime, allowed CPUs/NUMA, priority class, and co-tenancy constraints.
core_concepts.FrozenRunUse: Execution lease frozen at enqueue after RunCap and SchedContext checks. Runqueues must not store raw capability handles as authority.
core_concepts.EndpointCap: Resource-specific operation authority checked by resource endpoints such as VFS, socket, broker, device queue, or remote endpoint.
core_concepts.BudgetTicket: Bounded donated budget for broker/service execution, preventing service confused deputy and DoS amplification.
core_concepts.HyperTagMonitor: Small lower layer that enforces non-forgeable roots below Linux: domain registry, epochs, sealed run tokens, MemoryViews, IOMMU mappings, queue ownership, and root budgets.

accepted_invariants.text: No RunCap, no enqueue.
No SchedContext, no execution.
No budget, no execution.
No FrozenRunUse, no runqueue entry.
No valid epoch and generation, no execution.
No DomainTag activation, no context switch across domains.
No async work without caller provenance and frozen authority.
No caller budget, no broker/service execution on behalf of that caller.
Broker/service execution requires a live BudgetTicket reserved from caller budget.
Service authority alone must not execute caller work.
No FrozenEndpointUse, no async endpoint execution.
Linux credential override must not change CapSched DomainTag or frozen endpoint authority.
No raw pointer or mutable kernel object authority across Domain boundaries.
Linux-only prototypes must not claim hypervisor-grade isolation.
Mutable Linux DomainTag shadow state is not execution authority without monitor-owned activation.
No monitor RunToken, no active DomainTag or MemoryView for execution.
BPF and sched_ext may suggest or prototype policy, but must not be the production root for CapSched security invariants.
CapSched CPU placement must refine existing affinity, cpuset, sched-domain, housekeeping, and hotplug constraints rather than bypass them.
No cluster lease, mutable local shadow, or cached claim is directly executable; node-local execution and endpoint use require compiled LocalContext authority.
No live lease epoch, no node-local execution.
No monitor PageOwner, no Domain MemoryView mapping.
Linux page, memcg, slab, and page-cache metadata must not create cross-Domain memory authority.
Mutable page-cache data is not shared across Domains except through a typed service endpoint or sealed shared base.
Slab object lifetime or refcount validity is not capability authority without generation, epoch, and Domain checks.
Page revocation removes MemoryView mappings before the physical page is reused by another Domain.
Reclaim, writeback, and service memory work must carry caller provenance and a BudgetTicket when acting for a caller.
No Domain activation may carry untagged stale TLB translations into another Domain context.
No completed page revoke while MemoryView, direct-map, or TLB translations for that page remain.
Linux-visible direct-map shadow claims must not create hardware translation authority.
Mutable page-cache overlay state maps only into its owner Domain; sealed base content is the shareable form.
No stale overlay commit: page-cache overlay writeback requires provenance, ticket, current base version, and base-level serialization.
No QueueLease, no device queue doorbell or queue ownership transfer.
No monitor-owned IOMMU mapping, no DMA into a Domain MemoryView.
Interrupt delivery must be owned by the same Domain/service endpoint as the leased queue.
Linux shadow queue and IOMMU state must not create authority without a live monitor QueueTag, IOMMU map, and epoch.
Queue revocation invalidates submit, completion delivery, IOMMU map, in-flight DMA, and IRQ route together.
Queue rate or budget exhaustion prevents further doorbells even if Linux queue state says the queue is available.
No IRQ route aliasing across non-free queues.

Modern NIC QueueLease submit, completion, control, representor, and service paths must preserve typed operation class; SKB, XDP, AF_XDP, QueueControl, RepresentorForward, and ServiceWork authority must not collapse into one generic queue or worker authority.

XDP_TX page-pool reuse and AF_XDP zero-copy submit require explicit memory ownership authority; DMA-capable packet memory must not be authorized by queue reachability, SKB authority, generic XDP authority, or ambient driver state.

Devlink/rate/scheduler/VF/SF/representor lifecycle authority is QueueControl authority, and representor transmit requires RepresentorForward authority plus a live lower QueueLease; neither may be authorized by RunCap or plain netdev reachability.

SchedExecLease repair is never authority: a generation mismatch remains fail-closed until one bounded recovery quantum completes a final acquire-generation/state/contribution recheck, and a current stop request requires a later distinct scheduler observation rather than treating need-resched as a monitor or completion receipt.

design_boundaries: Do not decide exact Linux patch points before reading current upstream code.
Do not make RunCap a universal capability.
Do not place VFS, BPF, GPU, or protocol-specific semantics inside the scheduler.
Do not use sched_ext as the production security boundary; it is useful for policy exploration.
Do not claim hypervisor replacement without monitor-enforced memory, CPU, IOMMU, queue, epoch, and domain roots.
Do not treat validation as pass/fail plumbing; validation must support semantic security claims.
Do not treat TLC completion as the project objective; use TLC as one source of semantic evidence for the security and efficiency goals.
Every future Linux patch must name the assurance claim and gate it supports.

Every implementation slice, including L0, must preserve the shape of the long-horizon monitor-backed CapSched-H target; a thin Linux hook is acceptable only when it does not weaken final invariants or hide authority in Linux-global mutable state.

open_questions.text: Which upstream Linux version and exact commit should be used as the base?
open_questions.answer: Use git.kernel.org torvalds/linux at commit 4edcdefd4083ae04b1a5656f4be6cd83ae919ef4 on work branch capsched-linux-l0.

open_questions.text: What is the smallest L0 slice that can demonstrate RunCap/SchedContext/FrozenRunUse without destabilizing scheduler fast paths?
open_questions.answer: Slice 0A was accepted only as inert CONFIG_CAPSCHED scaffolding. No behavior-changing RunCap/SchedContext/FrozenRunUse slice is accepted yet.

open_questions.text: Which formal model should be written first: enqueue/pick/tick budget semantics, epoch revocation, or async provenance?
open_questions.answer: Start with the Runnable Lease Model: Task, TaskGeneration, ProcessGeneration, Domain, DomainEpoch, RunCap, SchedContext, FrozenRunUse, RunqueueState, CPU, and Budget. Keep async and monitor placeholders explicit.

open_questions.text: How should cluster-wide signed resource leases compile into local node SchedContexts and endpoint capabilities?
Should early io_uring endpoint provenance use direct CONFIG_CAPSCHED fields in io_kiocb/io_rsrc_node or side tables keyed by request/resource addresses?
Should registered endpoint revocation use eager cancellation or lazy execute-time rejection in Linux L0?
Should Linux L0 caller epoch revocation for broker tickets forfeit unspent reserved caller budget, refund it, or make that policy endpoint-specific?
open_questions.answer: The checked Broker BudgetTicket model uses strict caller-revoke forfeiture and service-revoke refund as a deliberately explicit baseline, not yet an accepted Linux policy.

open_questions.text: Should the first Linux Slice 0B introduce type-only endpoint/broker/domain authority scaffolding, or should cluster lease compilation be modeled first?
open_questions.answer: Model cluster lease compilation first, but do not gate progress on one giant full TLC run. The full ClusterLease systemd run was stopped after 17127406139 generated states, 550525279 distinct states, and no invariant error before interruption; decomposed ShadowForgery and EpochRevoke models then passed TLC.

open_questions.text: Should device isolation begin from VFIO/iommufd device assignment or from typed queue leases?
open_questions.answer: Begin from typed QueueLease semantics. Analysis 0016 says VFIO/iommufd is a compatibility substrate, while production authority is monitor-owned QueueTag, IOMMU map, IRQ route, epoch, and rate/budget. Formal 0011 QueueLease passed two TLC runs for the generic boundary; device-specific endpoint models remain future gates.

open_questions.text: Should TLC completion be treated as a project goal or as auxiliary validation evidence?
open_questions.answer: TLC is auxiliary evidence. The project goal is secure, efficient CapSched Domain separation. Large TLC runs should be split by semantic proof obligation rather than allowed to dominate design progress.

open_questions.text: Can Linux page metadata, memcg, or slab/page-cache state serve as the production Domain memory authority root?
open_questions.answer: No. Analysis 0017 says these are valuable accounting, lifetime, reclaim, and compatibility substrates, but production memory authority requires monitor-owned PageOwner and MemoryView mappings.

open_questions.text: Which remaining L2 memory risk should be modeled after MemoryOwnership?
open_questions.answer: Direct-map visibility and TLB revocation were modeled first. The first DirectMapTLB run found a stale TLB-on-Domain-switch counterexample; after requiring Domain activation to flush or retag translations, TLC completed with no invariant errors. Page-cache overlay conflict semantics remain a separate L2 gate.

open_questions.text: What page-cache overlay rule prevents stale Domain dirty state from overwriting a newer sealed base?
open_questions.answer: PageCacheOverlay found that per-overlay commit is insufficient. Commit/writeback requires provenance, ticket, current base version, and base-level serialization or an equivalent commit token.

open_questions.text: Should exec increment the scheduler-visible process generation for RunCap/FrozenRunUse validity?
open_questions.answer: Analysis 0029 leaves this undecided. Option A increments endpoint/object process generation but not scheduler RunCap generation. Option B increments scheduler-visible process generation and needs a current-exec revalidation model so execution does not become stale mid-exec.

open_questions.text: Where is the production root for CPU budget enforcement?
open_questions.answer: Analysis 0028 says Linux tick/update_curr paths are L0 measurement and compatibility substrates only. CapSched-H needs monitor-owned root Domain budget plus sealed SchedContext epoch and bounded overrun enforcement.

open_questions.text: Can a future CapSched runnable-admission check fail after Linux writes TASK_WAKING?
open_questions.answer: No ordinary fail-return is allowed after TASK_WAKING without a separate rollback/quarantine proof. Analysis 0030 and formal/0013 show the conservative boundary: fail-capable admission belongs before TASK_WAKING; post-TASK_WAKING checks are nofail assertions, fail-closed stops, or separately proven rollback paths.

open_questions.text: Where should resumable-run or endpoint-derived wake authority be prepared before F1, wake_q_add(), or try_to_wake_up()?
open_questions.answer: Analysis 0032 answers the general placement rule: generic wake/F1 cannot discover authority. Ordinary sleeps should use task-local resumable-run state; endpoint/shared-futex waits need waiter or endpoint-specific carriers; workqueue/kthread_work need work item carriers; kthreads need service-domain task authority; signal send remains ThreadControlCap while target wake uses resumable-run authority.

open_questions.text: Should the first behavior-changing L0 runnable slice use only task-local resumable-run state, or also introduce a minimal workqueue/kthread_work carrier?
How should shared futex cross-Domain waits and wakes be represented as endpoint authority instead of plain task-resume authority?
How should priority inheritance, RT lock wake states, and ww_mutex wound/wake semantics be separated from RunCap and SchedContext authority?
Should the first task-local run-state implementation use embedded hot fields, a pointer allocated in fork, or a hybrid hot/cold split?
open_questions.answer: Analysis 0033 leaves storage undecided. Embedded hot state avoids F1 allocation but grows task_struct; a pointer keeps task_struct smaller but still has a raw-copy hazard and exact fork-failure cleanup requirements. A hybrid hot/cold split is a candidate.

open_questions.text: Should successful exec bump process_generation for run authority, endpoint authority only, or both?
open_questions.answer: Analysis 0033 preserves the current default that exec does not implicitly change DomainTag or SchedContext. If process_generation changes across exec, run continuation and endpoint/object uses need separate refresh semantics.

open_questions.text: What is the class-specific stale-queued-task behavior for CFS, RT, deadline, sched_ext, core scheduling, and proxy-execution paths?
How does the current self-wake path prove it is using current-owned continuation rather than a normal fail-capable wake path?
Which first subsystem should exercise caller-derived work wrappers: synthetic validation work, io_uring fallback work, net work, or a broker/service test endpoint?
open_questions.answer: Analysis 0034 leaves the first subsystem undecided. Synthetic work is safest for proving carrier mechanics; io_uring and net are more realistic but have larger endpoint semantics.

open_questions.text: Should delayed caller-derived work reserve budget at submit time or use a local preauthorized reservation object at timer fire?
What merge semantics are acceptable for single-work coalescing patterns such as schedule-once-to-process-many-events?
open_questions.answer: Analysis 0034 rejects carrier overwrite while pending unless explicit multi-caller merge, accounting, revocation, and settlement rules are modeled for the subsystem.

open_questions.text: Which workqueue categories qualify as kernel-internal exceptions in L0, and how are they audited so exceptions do not swallow user-derived work?
How far should CapSched-H go in replacing the internal async substrate rather than wrapping existing workqueue/kthread_work users?
open_questions.answer: Analysis 0034 says deep redesign is acceptable and likely necessary for production, but the replacement must keep KernelCoreWork, ServiceMaintenanceWork, DomainRequestWork, MergedDomainBatchWork, InterruptDeferredWork, and ReclaimRescueWork typed separately instead of inventing ambient caller authority.

open_questions.text: Should same-Domain private futexes bypass FutexEndpoint objects in L0, or should all futexes pass through a uniform endpoint abstraction?
What exact errno or fail action should CapSched use for denied shared futex endpoint operations while preserving userspace compatibility?
Where should waiter-side capsched_futex_wait_ctx live: futex_q field, stack-owned side object, or hash-bucket side table?
How should FUTEX_WAKE_OP's second-key write authority compose with FutexWakeCap?
Which PI futex paths must be separated into the PI/RT/ww_mutex authority model?
open_questions.answer: Analysis 0035 leaves PI futex, rt_mutex ownership, and priority donation to N-052 rather than folding them into FutexWakeCap or RunCap.
